quotes.text: The system isn’t broken. It was built this way.
Control is an illusion designed by those who fear chaos.
Every firewall is just a challenge waiting to be bypassed.
We stitch the fault lines of the world together with code, then watch the tapestry fray as soon as we step back.
We stare into the code like a preacher into a confession, trying to rewrite the sins of a world that never logged out.
Every password you remember is another door someone else is trying to open.
They don't need chains when they can convince you the cage is your career.
Funny how silence always knows my address before people do.
Time does not forgive hesitation; it simply buries it beneath the next second.
The cleanest exploit is the one that makes the victim believe they chose it.
Revolutions don't knock; they slip through the cracks everyone ignored.
Trust is just malware with better branding.
Power survives by teaching people to measure their own chains.
The network never sleeps because fear is always online.
They sell comfort by the subscription and bill you with your future.
You can disappear from the internet, but good luck disappearing from yourself.
A delayed decision is still a decision made in favor of someone else.
Reality keeps changing its checksum, and somehow I'm always the corrupted file.
The loudest rebellion is refusing to become the product they already priced.
Every city has a backdoor; you just have to stop trusting the map.
Control is most complete when it feels indistinguishable from destiny.
The deepest loneliness begins when even your own thoughts start asking for proof.
Corporations don't erase people; they overwrite them.
The clock is only honest with those willing to lose everything before it strikes.
Every hidden process leaves a trace, even the ones running inside your mind.
